features.name: Dependency Injection
features.description: Hierarchical dependency injection system using Depends() for automatic resolution of function parameters.

features.name: Request Validation
features.description: Automatic request body, query, and path parameter validation using Pydantic models and type hints.

features.name: OpenAPI Generation
features.description: Automatic OpenAPI 3.x schema generation from route definitions and type annotations.

features.name: Middleware System
features.description: Starlette-based middleware pipeline supporting ASGI middleware and custom request/response interceptors.

features.name: WebSocket Support
features.description: Native WebSocket endpoint handling with dependency injection and connection lifecycle management.

features.name: Background Tasks
features.description: Built-in background task system for running operations after the response has been sent.

features.name: Security Schemes
features.description: Pluggable security schemes including OAuth2, API keys, and HTTP basic auth with automatic OpenAPI documentation.

features.name: Response Model
features.description: Type-safe response serialization and filtering using Pydantic models with include/exclude field control.

call_chain.role: Main dependency resolver that walks the dependency tree, builds sub-dependencies, and injects resolved values.
Analyzes a callable's signature to build a Dependant object describing all its parameter dependencies.
Marker class that wraps a dependency callable with optional use_cache flag for singleton behavior.
Creates the actual request handler that calls solve_dependencies before invoking the user's endpoint function.
Handles generator-based dependencies (yield dependencies) for setup/teardown patterns like database sessions.

dependency_injection.explanation: FastAPI's dependency injection works by analyzing function signatures at startup. When a route is registered, get_dependant() inspects the endpoint's parameters and builds a tree of Dependant objects. Each Depends() marker wraps a callable that will be resolved at request time. When a request arrives, solve_dependencies() walks the dependency tree bottom-up, resolving each dependency and caching results (when use_cache=True) to avoid duplicate calls. Generator dependencies (using yield) are handled specially by solve_generator(), which runs setup code before the endpoint and teardown code after the response, enabling patterns like database session management.

call_chain.role: Extracts and validates request body data against Pydantic models defined in the endpoint signature.
Validates query parameters and path parameters against their type annotations and Field constraints.
Catches RequestValidationError and returns a structured 422 JSON response with error details.
Converts Pydantic models and Python objects to JSON-serializable dicts for response encoding.

request_validation.explanation: Request validation leverages Python type hints and Pydantic. During route registration, FastAPI inspects endpoint parameters to determine which are path params, query params, headers, or body fields. At request time, request_params_to_args() extracts and validates path/query parameters, while request_body_to_args() parses the JSON body into Pydantic models. If validation fails, Pydantic raises a ValidationError which FastAPI catches and transforms into a 422 response with detailed error locations. This provides automatic, type-safe input validation with zero boilerplate in the endpoint function.

call_chain.role: Builds the complete OpenAPI schema dict by iterating over all routes and assembling paths, components, and info.
Generates the OpenAPI path item for a single route including parameters, request body, and response schemas.
Creates unique operation IDs from the route's endpoint function name and path.
Lazy property that generates and caches the OpenAPI schema on first access.

openapi_generation.explanation: OpenAPI generation happens lazily on first request to /openapi.json. FastAPI.openapi() calls get_openapi() which iterates over all registered routes. For each route, get_openapi_path() introspects the endpoint's Dependant tree to extract parameter schemas, request body models, and response models. Pydantic models are converted to JSON Schema and collected into the components/schemas section. The result is a complete OpenAPI 3.x specification that powers the interactive Swagger UI and ReDoc documentation pages served at /docs and /redoc.
